Example: 2

p.note: If less than 24 hours, count as (0) days.

entry.p: Displays the type of review performed based on the time when Initial Review was performed.
Example: Concurrent Review, Retrospective Review.

note.p: Concurrent Review is displayed if Initial Review is performed before patient's discharge.
Retrospective Review is displayed if Initial Review is performed after patient's discharge.

entry.p: Displays the Initial reviews from the initials raised by the CDS
Displays the Initial for the Initial reviews (Both standard and custom) selected when CDS raises the initial.
Example: Financial Initial, Quality Initial, Both Initial
Do not use.
Use 'Concurrent Initial Reviews' field under Review Metrics
Do not use.
Use 'Retrospective Initial Reviews' field under Review Metrics.
Displays average daily initial reviews performed by the CDS for the selected Activity Date range.
Initial Reviews Per Day = (Total number of Initial Reviews with the Initial Review Date in the selected activity date range/ No. of days CDS worked in the selected activity date range )

row.entry: Initial Reviews Per Day_Initial Reviews = (Total number of Initial Reviews with the Initial Review Date in the selected activity date range/ No. of days worked_Initial Reviews ). Rounded to 0 decimal places.

entry.p: Displays the count of Initial Reviews performed during Weekdays.
Example: 18

p.note: Weekdays = Monday (12AM) - Friday (5:59 PM)

entry.p: Displays the Initial Review Date.
Example: 12/2/2020
Displays the Initial Review Date in EST Timezone.
Example: 7/5/2020
Displays the Initial Review date and time in EST Timezone.

row.entry: Do not use. Use 'Initial Review TAT (Hours)' instead.
Displays the Daily Objectives for Review TAT in hours.

entry.p: Displays the total count of Initial Reviews performed on an encounter including Weekdays.
Example: 25
Displays the ADDITIONAL review from the is raised by the CDS
Displays the ADDITIONAL for the IS review (Both standard and custom) selected when CDS raises the is.
Example: Financial ADDITIONAL, Quality ADDITIONAL, Both ADDITIONAL
Displays the total number of days CDS has worked for in the selected activity date range.
If a CDS has at least an action performed (Initial Review or Additional Review) on a given date, it is counted as a "day worked".

ul.li: All days of the week (including weekends) are considered.
Encounters with "Review Not Needed" encounter status are excluded from this metric calculation.

row.entry: If a CDS has at least one initial review on a given date, it is counted as a "No. of days worked_Initial Reviews".In the existing Measure "No. of days worked", both initial and additional reviews are considered.

entry.p: Displays the list of Payers.
Example: Payer Description (Payer ID)
Displays boolean value if Retrospective review is performed or no.
Example: True, False

note.p: True is displayed if Retrospective review is performed on the encounter.
False is displayed if Retrospective review is not performed on the encounter.

entry.p: Displays the count of additional reviews done retrospectively.
Example: 45

note.p: Concurrent Additional Review: Additional Review done prior to or on discharge.
Discharge Date/time >Additional Review Date/time or Additional Review Date/time = Discharge Date/time
Encounters with "Review Not Needed" encounter status are excluded from this metric calculations.

entry.p: Displays the count of initial reviews done retrospectively.
Example: 19

note.p: Retrospective Initial Review: Initial Review done after discharge.
Initial Review Date/time > Discharge Date/time.
Encounters with "Review Not Needed" encounter status are excluded from this metric calculations.

entry.p: Displays the sum of Initial and Additional reviews performed retrospectively.
Example: 64

p.note: Encounters with "Review Not Needed" encounter status are excluded from this metric calculations.

entry.p: Displays the User ID of the CDS performing encounter review.
Example: earlm
Displays out of total encounters (Admissions/Discharges), the percentage of encounters reviewed.
Review Rate= Encounters Reviewed (Initial Reviews) / Total encounters (Admissions/Discharges) x 100. (Rounded to 0 decimal places.)
Example: 89%

p.note: Encounters with "Review Not Needed" encounter status are excluded from this metric calculations.

entry.p: Displays the type of review performed based on the time when Initial Review was performed.
Example: Concurrent Review, Retrospective Review.

note.p: Concurrent Review is displayed if Initial Review is performed before patient's discharge.
Retrospective Review is displayed if Initial Review is performed after patient's discharge.

entry.p: Displays the type of review performed.
Example: Initial Review, Additional Review

row.entry: Displays the distinct count of encounters that have been reviewed.

entry.p: Displays the list of sources.
Example: NG_CDI, CLIN_CDI etc.
Displays the suffix for the default timezone configured.
Example: ET is displayed if Eastern Time is the default timezone configured.

row.entry: Do not use. Will be removed in future releases.

entry.p: Displays the sum of Initial and Additional reviews performed for the reviewed encounters.
Example: 85

p.note: Encounters with "Review Not Needed" encounter status are excluded from this metric calculations.

entry.p: Displays average daily total reviews performed by the CDS for the selected Activity Date range

Total Reviews Per Day = (Total number of Initial and Additional Reviews with the Initial Review Date and additional Review Date in the selected activity date range/ No. of days CDS worked in the selected activity date range)

ul.li: Additional Review Date/Time falls in the selected activity date range.
Initial Review Date/Time falls in the selected activity date range.
Initial and Additional Reviews for Encounters with "Review Not Needed" encounter status are excluded from this metric calculations.

entry.p: Displays the sum of Initial and Additional reviews performed for the reviewed encounters during Weekdays.
Example: 75

note.p: Weekdays = Monday (12AM) - Friday (5:59 PM), i.e. Weekend Only Stay = N
Encounters with "Review Not Needed" encounter status are excluded from this metric calculations.

row.entry: Total Reviews Per Day = (Total number of Initial and Additional Reviews with the Initial Review Date and additional Review Date in the selected activity date range/ No. of days worked_Initial Reviews). Rounded to 0 decimal places.

entry.p: Displays the list of Units available .
Example: CARD, TELE, UNIT1 etc.
Displays the description of the Specialty as configured in the Specialties configuration screen.
Example: Cardiology
Displays the Specialty description and ID as configured in the Specialties configuration screen.
Example: Specialty Description (Specialty ID)
Displays the Specialty ID as configured in the Specialties configuration screen.
Example: CARD

row.entry: Out of the total CLU suggested codes, this column displays the count of Diagnosis codes that display CC code indicator.These codes are available under the Suggested section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant

Displays the count of CLU Suggested Diagnosis codes that display CC, MCC, Target Diagnosis code.
This count should not include any Quality Diagnosis codes.
These are the codes available under the Suggested section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant.

Displays the count of CLU Suggested Diagnosis codes that display CC, MCC, Target Diagnosis, Quality diagnosis codes.These codes are available under the Suggested section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant.

Out of the total CLU suggested codes, this column displays the count of Diagnosis codes that display ELX code indicator.These codes are available under the Suggested section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant.

Out of the total CLU suggested codes, this column displays the count of Diagnosis codes that display MCC code indicator.These are the codes available under the Suggested section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant.
Displays non CC, MCC, Target Diagnosis and Quality Diagnosis codes.These codes are available under the Other section in the Diagnosis.
Displays the count of procedure codes that are suggested by the engine.These are the codes available under the Suggested section in the Procedures category in CDS Assistant.
Displays the count of PCS procedure codes that are suggested by the engine.These are the codes available under the Suggested section in the Procedures category in CDS Assistant.
Displays the count of SNOMED procedure codes that are suggested by the engine.These are the codes available under the Suggested section in the Procedures category in CDS Assistant.

Displays the count of CLU Suggested Quality Diagnosis codes related to potential HAC, PSI, PDI, NQI or Core measure opportunity.
This count should not include any other CC, MCC, Target Diagnosis.
These are the codes available under the Quality section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant.

Out of the total CLU suggested codes, the Diagnosis codes that display CC code indicator.
These odes are available under the Suggested section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant.
Multiple values are comma separated.

Out of the total CLU suggested codes, the Diagnosis codes that display CC, MCC, Target Diagnosis code.
This field should not include any Quality Diagnosis codes.
These are the codes available under the Suggested section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant.
Multiple values are displayed comma separated.

Out of the total CLU suggested codes, the Diagnosis codes that display CC, MCC, Target Diagnosis, Quality diagnosis codes.
These are the codes available under the Suggested section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant.
Multiple values are displayed comma separated."

These codes are available under the Suggested section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant.
Multiple values are comma separated.

Out of the total CLU suggested codes, the Diagnosis codes that display MCC code indicator.
These codes are available under the Suggested section in the Diagnosis category in CDS Assistant.
Multiple values are comma separated.

Out of the total CLU suggested codes, the diagnosis codes that display non CC, MCC, Target Diagnosis and Quality Diagnosis codes.
These are the codes available under the Other section in the Diagnosis.

The Procedure codes that are suggested by the engine.

Multiple Procedure codes are displayed comma separated.

The SNOMED Procedure codes that are suggested by the engine.

Multiple Procedure codes are displayed comma separated.

Displays primary reconciliation type and other reconciliation type values.

entry.p: Displays the list of available units.
Example: CARD, ICU, IM etc.
Displays the Address of the user as configured in 'Address Line 1' under Contact Information in the Users configuration screen.
Example: 1/84 Street 1
Displays the Address of the user as configured in 'Address Line 2' under Contact Information in the Users configuration screen.
Example: Golf Street, 42
Displays the Address of the user as configured in 'Address Line 3' under Contact Information in the Users configuration screen.
Example: California- 123456
Displays the City information of the user as configured under Contact Information in the Users configuration screen.
Example: Texas
Displays the Contact Email information of the user as configured under Contact Information in the Users configuration screen.
Example: abc.def@xyz.com
Displays if the Contact email has been verified.
Example: Yes , No

p.note: Yes is displayed in case of verified Contact Email.

entry.p: Displays the Country information of the user as configured under Contact Information in the Users configuration screen.
Example: USA
Displays the Department information of the user as configured in the Users configuration screen.
Example:Cardiology
Displays the Email Address of the user as configured in the Users configuration screen.
Example: abc.def@xyz.com
Displays the EMR Login ID of the user as configured under EMR Information in the Users configuration screen.
Example: 123456
Displays the list of facilities along with Facility ID user has access to as configured in the Users configuration screen.
Example: Facility A (FACA), Facility B (FACB), Facility C (FACC) etc.

p.note: Facilities are displayed comma separated for users with access to multiple facilities.

entry.p: Displays the First Name of the User.
Example: FIRST
Displays the Last Name of the User.
Example: LAST
Displays the location of the user as configured in the Users configuration screen.
Example: 123456
Displays the Middle Name of the User as configured in the Users configuration screen.
Example: Middle
Displays the Phone information of the user as configured under Contact Information in the Users configuration screen.
Displays the list of roles assigned to the user as configured in the Users configuration screen.
Example: CDS_MANAGER, FACILITY_ADMIN

p.note: Multiple roles assigned to the user are displayed comma separated.

entry.p: Displays the State information of the user as configured under Contact Information in the Users configuration screen.
Example: Georgia
Displays the status of the User.
Example: Active, Inactive
Displays the Title/Credentials of the User as configured in the Users configuration screen.
Example: Dr.
Displays the User ID as configured in the Users configuration screen.
Example: abcdef
Displays the User Type as configured in the Users configuration screen.
Example: Regular User, Nuance User, Nuance Service Account.
Displays the Zip Code information of the user as configured under Contact Information in the Users configuration screen.
Example: 123456

row.entry: Do not use. Will be removed in future releases.

entry.p: Displays the Working/Possible diagnosis/procedure codes added in the encounter.
Example: R52, L89123
Displays the code description of the Working/Possible diagnosis/procedure codes added in the encounter.
Example: Salmonella sepsis, Hypo-osmolality and hyponatremia
Displays the code type of the Working/Possible diagnosis/procedure codes added in the encounter.
Example: CM, PCS
Do not use.
Displays the code indicators assigned to Working/Possible diagnosis/procedure added in the encounter.
Example: CC, MCC, HAC etc.
Displays the Present on Admission (POA) value added to the Working/Possible diagnosis/procedure in the encounter.
Example: Y, N, 1 etc
Displays the status of the Quality Alert triggered for the Working/Possible diagnosis/procedure added in the encounter.
Example: Salmonella sepsis, Hypo-osmolality and hyponatremia
Displays the review where the diagnosis/procedure reviews are added in the encounter.
Example: WORKING, POSSIBLE
Displays the sequence number of diagnosis/procedure seqs added in the encounter in Working/Possible reviews.
Example: 1, 2, 3 etc.

p.note: SEQ_NUM is displayed if the CM/PCS code is added in the 2nd position in Working/Possible Reviews.
